edges.description: Glycosidase deficiency leads to lysosomal accumulation of undegraded glycan substrates.
edges.source: Lysosomal Glycosidase Deficiency and Impaired Glycoprotein Catabolism

edges.description: Lysosomal storage drives CNS glial activation and neuronal loss.
Undegraded oligosaccharide substrates are excreted in urine.
Somatic glycoprotein storage contributes to coarse facial features.
Skeletal storage pathology produces dysostosis multiplex.
Chronic neuronal dysfunction and loss underlie intellectual disability.
Microglial activation and neuronal loss produce progressive neurodegeneration.
Neurodegenerative CNS involvement can manifest with seizures.

nodes.description: Coarse facies are a recurrent somatic feature across the oligosaccharidoses (fucosidosis exemplar).

evidence.snippet: growth retardation, coarse facial features, hepatosplenomegaly, telangiectasis or angiokeratomas, epilepsy, inguinal hernia, and dysostosis multiplex

nodes.description: Skeletal dysplasia (dysostosis multiplex) accompanies the somatic storage.

evidence.snippet: growth retardation, coarse facial features, hepatosplenomegaly, telangiectasis or angiokeratomas, epilepsy, inguinal hernia, and dysostosis multiplex

nodes.description: Progressive intellectual disability is a shared neurological feature across the group.

evidence.reference_title: Fucosidosis: A Review of a Rare Disease.
evidence.snippet: leading to progressive intellectual disability, learning difficulties

nodes.description: Deficiency of a lysosomal glycosidase (e.g., alpha-L-fucosidase, alpha-mannosidase, aspartylglucosaminidase, sialidase) blocks the stepwise lysosomal degradation of N- and O-linked glycans of glycoproteins, glycolipids, and oligosaccharides.
nodes.id: Lysosomal Glycosidase Deficiency and Impaired Glycoprotein Catabolism

evidence.reference_title: Fucosidosis: A Review of a Rare Disease.
evidence.snippet: This enzyme is responsible for breaking down fucose-containing glycoproteins, glycolipids, and oligosaccharides within the lysosome

nodes.description: Undegraded oligosaccharides and glycopeptides accumulate in lysosomes, which become engorged with substrate, with secondary metabolic and downstream cellular consequences.

evidence.reference_title: Fucosidosis: A Review of a Rare Disease.
evidence.snippet: leading to the accumulation of fucose-rich substrates in lysosomes. Lysosomes become engorged with undigested substrates

nodes.description: Progressive neurodegeneration from chronic lysosomal dysfunction and neuroinflammation.

evidence.reference_title: Fucosidosis: A Review of a Rare Disease.
evidence.snippet: neuronal loss, leading to the neurodegenerative symptoms

nodes.description: CNS lysosomal dysfunction causes microglial activation, neuroinflammation, and neuronal loss, producing the progressive neurodegenerative features of the oligosaccharidoses.

evidence.reference_title: Fucosidosis: A Review of a Rare Disease.
evidence.snippet: The central nervous system is particularly vulnerable, with lysosomal dysfunction causing microglial activation, inflammation, and neuronal loss

nodes.description: Urinary excretion of undegraded oligosaccharides / glycopeptides is the shared biochemical hallmark and screening abnormality of this disease group.

evidence.reference_title: A 37-Year-Old Man With Intellectual Disability Discovered to Have Aspartylglucosaminuria: Implications for the Diagnosis of Genetic Causes.
evidence.snippet: accumulation of aberrant metabolites in the patient's urine

nodes.description: Epilepsy/seizures occur in the neurodegenerative oligosaccharidoses.

evidence.snippet: telangiectasis or angiokeratomas, epilepsy, inguinal hernia
